events_with_summaries.title: Dept 95: Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP) GRS/Computer Assisted Ordering (CAO)
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: Department 95 and Backroom: Starting May 3, Keurig Dr Pepper orders will move to system-generated ordering through GRS, aligning with standard grocery processes. Early execution is critical, as the system takes two to three weeks to stabilize, so accurate on-hands, team alignment, and not refusing orders will directly impact in-stock. This applies to KDP company-owned stores only, with reps continuing to support and adjust as needed.

events_with_summaries.title: May/June Profit Protection Framework Update
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: Profit Protection is expanding into more modulars through June. Use the lookup tool to confirm whether it applies to your store, then unlock the identified categories and set modulars as drawn, using the required AP fixtures where noted.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 10: ACC Filters Modular Transition Update
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: Dept. 10: This update walks stores through the oil and air filters modular transition, including DSL labeling steps, FRAM consolidation, upright air filter merchandising and support resources ahead of the Week 37 Reset.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 12: New 8 oz Paint Sample Tray
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: Dept. 12: Paint sample display tray stocked with 8 oz paint samples that can be tinted for customers. Use the tinting and shaking instructions that match your store’s software.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 12: Week 15 Modular Expansion
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: Dept. 12: Week 15 Bath, Kitchen and Plumbing modular relay changes include space updates and 31 new items launching, including nationally branded items and filtered shower heads.

events_with_summaries.title: Backroom: Join the DSD Office Hours for Real-Time Support
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: Backroom: DSD Office Hours provide fast, direct support for Direct Store Delivery issues by connecting stores with key partners every other Tuesday. The next session is May 12 from 2:00 to 3:00 p.m. Central on Teams, with ongoing sessions available for continued support.

events_with_summaries.title: D8: National Pet Month Signage
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: For National Pet Month, stores will receive in‑store signage highlighting pet savings, services, and convenience across Pet and Floor Care. Signage arrives starting May 2 and should be set May 1 through June 1 in designated Pet and Floor Care locations.

events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: An exclusive Olive & June x Star Wars Nail Care sidekick is arriving in select Dept. 46 locations for Star Wars Day. Stores should place the display in a high‑visibility, easy‑to‑find location and keep it well maintained to drive engagement and sales.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 40: New Wellness Minis Endcap Has Arrived
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: In department 40, the new Wellness Minis endcap is coming to stores in week 16, offering low cost trial size wellness items to drive impulse add ons, bringing new customers into the category, and encouraging repeat purchases as shoppers trade up to full size. Set the pre-filled display upon arrival. Insert price labels. Keep it stocked and zoned. Do not substitute items. Add aisle location for online pickup and delivery associates to locate.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 40: Pusher Kits for Upcoming Mods
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: In department 40, modular resets are beginning over the next couple of weeks which include foot care, vitamins, and digestive wellness. To make sure modulars are set to the most current planograms and deliver the best customer experience, please have teams order pushers as needed. Review the message for fixture order numbers.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 79: Little Nest Travel Pillow and Blanket
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: Select stores are featuring the Little Nest Travel Pillow and Blanket for Toddlers in Dept. 79, set on a four-way fixture and live as of Week 10 (April 4–10). This Q1 feature adds a fun, seasonal brand to help make the department a go‑to destination for customers shopping for toddler travel essentials.

If they cannot fit seven across due to the size of the Digital Shelf Labels (DSL), they will need to choose a smaller DSL.

events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms

Be sure to show customers the value and thank you and your teams for all that you do to make our stores America's best place to shop!

events_with_summaries.title: Bakery Trainer Store Support
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: Soon, Bakery Trainers from one of our suppliers will visit stores soon to strengthen bakery routines, fine tune process execution, build associate skills, in an effort to elevate the overall customer experience. Trainers will take a hands on approach and work side-by-side with Bakery associates, Team Leads, and Coaches on the salesfloor to reinforce baking, decorating, general in-store production routines and standards for quality, food safety, and consistency. Afterward, they’ll recap with store leadership on observations and next steps. Please partner during the visit, ensure teams are available, and attend the post-visit recap.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 81: WK14 Bread Updates
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: In department 81, Commercial Bread, ensure teams work clean and continue setting the buns and rolls modulars this week. Expect to see changes as Sam's Choice and Sara Lee brands transition. Keep these key items full to help support your historical summer buns and rolls volume. Set, price and show the value on key Rollbacks to help boost sales Keep tortilla Rollbacks full and priced correctly to support Cinco de Mayo on Tuesday! Follow this quick store checklist to win in Commercial Bread!

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 93: Summer
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: May holidays will drive Meat demand. For Cinco de Mayo, expect lifts in ground beef. For Mother’s Day and Memorial Day expect them in steaks, ribs, lobster and snow crab. Act now to win summer grilling. Ensure department 93 precision modulars are reviewed and right-sized; unplug holes and correct on-hands to improve flow and reduce waste. Deep-clean cases and fixtures before stocking. Check product temps across the department daily. Finally, use the Store Feature Order Tool now to place feature orders; remember items have a 10-21 day lead time.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 94: Produce Updates
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: In department 94, Produce, from now through week 17 (May 2–29), stores serviced by six impacted DCs will see iceberg lettuce shortages: about 60% fill on core SKUs and 0% on select salad kits. Fill gaps with adjacent items and face out top sellers. Weather is tightening asparagus and tomato supply through week 19—verify on-hands and repurpose salsa bins for bulk tomatoes. Mother’s Day floral is in: confirm receipts, complete price changes, continue to condense action-alley displays while updating aisle locations and pick gently. Stone fruit season starts May 4, cherries run through June 24 and watermelons are coming.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 98: Spring Sell-Through Acceleration\n\nSpecial Bulletin
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: In department 98 Fresh Bakery, win Mother’s Day sales by following the approved decorating guidance, keeping the department full, fresh and clean, tables stocked with best-sellers and focus on key items such as angel food items and carrot, cheese and white cakes all week. Order donut ingredients now to stay in stock and avoid gaps. Drive value and volume with Rollbacks! Feature and flag Marketside mini muffin, 28 count cookies and angle food items.

events_with_summaries.title: Summer Grilling Basket Launch Plan
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: In Fresh, the Summer Grilling Basket program launches May 4 and sets through July 5, offering a family-of-8 meal solution for under $5 per person. New this year: 10 of 13 items are consolidated in one shoppable location between the frozen and refrigerated bunker endcaps and includes supporting sidekick fixtures and a 4-way to deliver a strong in-store grilling statement and elevate the customer experience!  Ensure the grilling 4-way, bunkers, endcaps and sidekicks are set to plan, items stocked, high standards are maintained and the Summer Grilling Basket signing kit is in place through July 5.

events_with_summaries.title: AI Tip of the Week: Drive AI Adoption in Your Store
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text:
Drive AI Adoption in Your Facility.

When teams build small, repeatable practices, they are more likely to adopt tools that improve efficiency and decision-making.

This approach focuses on helping leaders and associates use AI tools like ChatGPT and Gemini in practical, everyday ways. It provides two guided prompts. One for managers and one for people and learning leaders.

Managers are guided to identify barriers to AI adoption, create a short development plan, define real use cases and set clear accountability.

People and learning leaders focus on designing quick, practical training, making AI approachable, reinforcing learning, and measuring success.

The goal is simple: build confidence, drive consistent usage, and make AI a natural part of daily work.
